metaTitle: Request a W-9 From a Vendor | Keelstar
metaDescription: Send a tracked W-9 request link, validate IRS fields on submission, and keep a dated audit record before you pay the vendor.
h1: Request a W-9 from a vendor without email chaos
hero: Keelstar sends a secure Form W-9 request your vendor completes in a browser. Required fields are validated on submission so AP never files an incomplete tax record.
problem: Finance teams still ask for W-9s over generic email. Attachments get lost, legal names do not match payment records, and nobody can prove when the form was collected.
problemBullets: Untracked email requests stall when vendors ignore one-off messages
Unsigned or partial W-9s slip into shared drives without validation
No single list shows which active vendors still lack a current W-9
Auditors ask for request dates and versions AP cannot reconstruct

sections.title: Why a tracked request beats a PDF attachment
paragraphs: A secure request link tells you exactly who was asked, when they opened it, and when they submitted. Vendors do not need a Keelstar login—only a browser session to complete the IRS form.

sections.title: Tie W-9 collection to payment readiness
paragraphs: Block or flag invoices when the vendor record lacks a validated W-9. AP sees status on the vendor profile instead of searching inboxes before each 1099 season.
bullets: Branded request email with your organization name
Field validation before the W-9 is accepted
Automatic reminders until submission or escalation

sections.title: Evidence finance can export
paragraphs: Every request, reminder, and download is timestamped. Export the W-9 packet with its history when tax, audit, or vendor management asks for proof of due diligence.

whoItsFor: Accounts payable managers preparing for 1099 season
Vendor onboarding specialists collecting tax IDs at setup
Controllers who need backup withholding documentation
Healthcare and government contractors with strict vendor files
checklist: Every active vendor has a validated W-9 on file
Legal name and TIN were checked before first payment
Stale forms are flagged for re-collection before year-end
Request and submission history is exportable

faqs.q: Do vendors need a Keelstar account?
faqs.a: No. Vendors complete the W-9 through a secure link in their browser. Your team manages collection and monitoring inside Keelstar.

faqs.q: Can we request W-9s in bulk?
faqs.a: Yes. Upload or paste a vendor list and Keelstar sends individual tracked requests with shared reminder rules.
Keelstar checks required IRS fields—including legal name, tax classification, address, and TIN format—before accepting the submission.
You set the cadence. Keelstar emails non-responders until they submit, escalate, or you close the request.

faqs.q: Does this replace our W-9 PDF workflow?
faqs.a: It replaces untracked email PDFs with validated, versioned records tied to each vendor profile.

metaDescription: Collect W-9 forms online with a secure vendor link, field validation, and reminders until every submission is complete.
h1: Collect W-9 forms online with validated submissions
hero: Give vendors a secure browser form instead of email attachments. Keelstar validates IRS fields and stores each W-9 with version history.
problem: Teams email blank W-9 PDFs and hope vendors return complete forms. Online collection without validation recreates the same 1099 risk in a web form.
problemBullets: Vendors print, scan, or photograph W-9s with missing signatures
No validation means AP still accepts blank TIN fields
Email threads do not show who has not responded
Records are not tied to the vendor profile in your ERP

metaDescription: Manage vendor W-9 records in one workspace with status dashboards, reminders, and exportable audit history.
h1: Manage every vendor W-9 from one workspace
hero: See which vendors lack a current W-9, send reminders in bulk, and export a complete tax file history without spreadsheet tabs.
problem: W-9 status lives in spreadsheets that drift from the vendor master. AP discovers gaps only when a payment or 1099 is due.
problemBullets: Multiple versions of the same vendor W-9 in shared folders
No owner for re-collection after entity name changes
Bulk reminders are manual mail-merge projects
Auditors cannot get a single export of request history

metaDescription: W-9 collection software with secure vendor links, validation, reminders, and audit exports for AP and tax teams.
h1: W-9 collection software built for AP audit trails
hero: Replace inbox chasing with software that requests, validates, and monitors W-9s on every vendor profile.
problem: Spreadsheet trackers and shared folders are not software—they cannot validate fields or prove when a vendor was asked.
problemBullets: No central queue of outstanding W-9 requests
Validation rules differ by whoever opens the attachment
Reminder work falls on AP admins each quarter
1099 projects restart from zero every January

metaDescription: Automate W-9 reminders until vendors submit or your team escalates—with timestamps on every nudge.
h1: Automate W-9 reminders until vendors respond
hero: Set reminder cadence once. Keelstar emails non-responders, logs each nudge, and surfaces vendors who still block payment readiness.
problem: Manual follow-ups depend on someone remembering to forward the same email. Vendors learn they can ignore the first request.
problemBullets: Reminder history is not saved in AP inboxes
Managers cannot see which vendors are overdue
Escalation rules live in tribal knowledge
Year-end volume overwhelms small AP teams

metaDescription: Send bulk W-9 requests from a vendor list with individual secure links and shared reminder rules.
h1: Send bulk W-9 requests from your vendor list
hero: Paste or upload vendors and launch tracked W-9 requests in one action. Each vendor gets their own link; your team gets one status board.
problem: Mail-merge W-9 blasts look personal but provide no per-vendor status. AP still tracks responses manually.
problemBullets: One bounced email invalidates the whole mail merge batch
No per-vendor validation on return
Duplicate requests when two admins run parallel lists
Cannot export who responded for auditors

metaDescription: Validate W-9 fields before payment and flag vendors missing current tax documentation.
h1: Validate W-9s before you release payment
hero: Keelstar checks required IRS fields at submission and shows AP which vendors are not payment-ready because tax documentation is missing or stale.
problem: Payment systems rarely know if the W-9 on file is complete. Backup withholding issues surface after money has left the account.
problemBullets: Invoices approve while W-9 status is unknown
TIN typos are discovered during 1099 filing
Entity changes do not trigger re-collection
Finance learns about gaps from IRS notices, not software

metaDescription: Collect vendor tax documents beyond a single form—W-9, withholding certificates, and re-certifications with audit history.
h1: Collect vendor tax documents with one compliance record
hero: Centralize W-9 and related tax paperwork on the vendor profile so AP and tax share the same source of truth.
problem: Tax documents arrive as ad-hoc attachments with no link to the vendor master record or payment holds.
problemBullets: State withholding forms live outside the W-9 folder
Re-certification after mergers is manual
Auditors request packets assembled by hand
No reminder cycle for expiring tax certifications

metaDescription: Keep W-9 version control when vendors change legal names, entities, or TINs—with history on the vendor profile.
h1: W-9 version control when vendor details change
hero: Store every submitted W-9 with timestamps. When a vendor updates their information, prior versions remain available for tax and audit questions.
problem: Teams overwrite old W-9 files in shared drives, losing proof of what was on file when payments were made.
problemBullets: Only the latest PDF is kept with no history
1099 corrections cannot reference prior versions
Merger-related entity changes erase old records
Auditors ask what was on file at payment date

metaDescription: Get stale W-9 alerts before year-end and payment runs so AP re-collects outdated tax forms proactively.
h1: Stale W-9 alerts before year-end crunch
hero: Define how long a W-9 stays current. Keelstar flags stale records and launches re-collection so January is not a fire drill.
problem: Without age rules, W-9s sit for years while vendor legal names and TINs change in the ERP silently.
problemBullets: No policy for when to refresh W-9s
Stale records are invisible until 1099 season
Re-collection is a one-time project not a monitor
Payment continues against outdated tax data

metaDescription: COI tracking software to collect ACORD certificates, monitor expirations, and alert before vendor coverage lapses.
h1: COI tracking software that warns you before coverage lapses
hero: Track certificates of insurance in one system with expiration monitoring, requirement checks, and vendor self-service upload.
problem: Insurance certificates expire quietly in email. Operations discovers uninsured vendors only after an incident or audit.
problemBullets: Expiration dates are re-keyed into spreadsheets
No alert before the certificate lapses
Coverage limits are not compared to contract requirements
Additional insured endorsements are missing from files

metaDescription: COI expiration alerts with lead time so procurement and risk can renew vendor insurance before work stops.
h1: COI expiration alerts with lead time you control
hero: Set how far ahead to warn. Keelstar notifies owners and optionally vendors before certificates expire.
problem: Calendar reminders on individual certificates do not scale across hundreds of vendors and subcontractors.
problemBullets: Alerts live on personal calendars
Vendors are not prompted to upload renewals
Lapsed COIs do not block POs or site access
Risk learns about lapses from contractors, not software

metaDescription: ACORD 25 tracking for vendor certificates—capture limits, expiration, and holder information without manual data entry.
h1: Track ACORD 25 certificates without spreadsheet re-keying
hero: Keelstar reads expiration and coverage details from ACORD certificates so your team reviews requirements instead of typing dates.
problem: Risk staff spend hours transferring ACORD fields into Excel, introducing typos that hide lapses.
problemBullets: Manual entry errors on expiration dates
Limits are not validated against contracts
Multiple ACORD versions clutter inboxes
No link between certificate and vendor master

metaDescription: Vendor insurance certificate tracking tied to each vendor profile with renewal reminders and audit exports.
h1: Track vendor insurance certificates on every profile
hero: Every certificate stays on the vendor record with status, history, and reminders—visible to procurement, risk, and AP.
problem: Certificates are filed by vendor name in folders that do not match ERP spelling, so renewals attach to the wrong supplier.
problemBullets: Name mismatches hide lapsed coverage
Subcontractor COIs are separate from prime vendors
No status field for awaiting renewal
Audits require manual folder searches

metaDescription: Certificate of insurance tracker for operations teams who need current COIs before vendors step on site.

hero: Know which vendors are cleared to work today. Block or flag vendors whose certificates are expired or below required limits.
problem: Site supervisors call the office to ask if insurance is current because there is no shared tracker.
problemBullets: Field teams cannot self-serve COI status
Expired COIs are discovered at the gate
Contract limits are not enforced systematically
Incident response lacks proof of coverage

metaDescription: COI renewal reminders for vendors and internal owners so certificates are replaced before expiration.
h1: COI renewal reminders vendors actually respond to
hero: Automated renewal reminders include a secure upload link so vendors return updated certificates without a new email thread.
problem: Generic please renew emails lack upload paths, so vendors reply with unreadable photos of certificates.
problemBullets: Renewal requests lack secure upload links
Internal owners must chase the same vendors repeatedly
Reminder history is not documented
Renewed certificates are not linked to the old version

metaDescription: Manage insurance certificate expiration dates across your vendor roster with dashboards and escalation rules.
h1: Manage insurance certificate expiration at scale
hero: Dashboards show certificates expiring this week, this month, and already lapsed—sorted by vendor criticality.
problem: Expiration reporting is a pivot table that is outdated the moment it is exported.
problemBullets: No live view of expiring coverage
Critical vendors are not prioritized
Lapsed certificates stay active in the ERP
Executives see insurance risk only after incidents

metaDescription: Verify vendor liability insurance limits and endorsements against your contracts before approving spend or access.
h1: Verify vendor liability insurance against your contracts
hero: Compare general liability, auto, and umbrella limits to what your MSAs require. Flag vendors who fall short before they perform work.
problem: Contracts specify insurance requirements that nobody compares to the actual certificate until litigation.
problemBullets: Required limits are not checked automatically
Umbrella and auto coverages are skipped in review
Underinsured vendors still receive POs
Additional insured language is not verified

metaDescription: COI requirements tracking that maps contract insurance clauses to each vendor certificate on file.
h1: Track COI requirements from contract to certificate
hero: Store what each vendor type must carry and compare incoming ACORD forms to those rules automatically.
problem: Insurance requirements live in PDF contracts while certificates are reviewed in isolation.
problemBullets: Contract clauses are not operationalized
Different vendor types share one generic checklist
Waivers and exceptions are not logged
Renewals reset the review from scratch

metaDescription: Collect additional insured endorsements on vendor certificates and prove your organization is named correctly.
h1: Collect additional insured endorsements with proof
hero: Request certificates that name your organization as additional insured and verify endorsement language before vendors start work.
problem: Operations assumes the COI includes additional insured language because the contract says so.
problemBullets: Endorsements are missing on otherwise valid COIs
Certificate holder box is blank or wrong
No workflow to reject incomplete certificates
Renewals drop additional insured without review

metaDescription: Run an OIG exclusion check on vendors and individuals with logged results for healthcare compliance programs.

problemBullets: New vendors are not screened before first invoice
Individuals paid as contractors are skipped
Match decisions are not recorded
Payer audits ask for screening dates you cannot produce

metaDescription: OIG LEIE search workflows for compliance teams who need repeatable searches and audit trails.

hero: Replace ad-hoc LEIE lookups with scheduled searches tied to each vendor and individual on your roster.

hero: Automate LEIE screening across your vendor and contractor population with reminders when screening ages out.

problemBullets: Screening cadence is inconsistent by department

Screening dates are not visible on vendor profiles

problemBullets: No proof the monthly run completed

metaDescription: Vendor setup portal for suppliers to submit W-9, insurance, and onboarding forms without a Keelstar account.

metaDescription: Vendor information management that keeps tax, insurance, screening, and contracts current on one profile.

metaDescription: Contract risk review software to flag indemnity, liability, and termination clauses before you sign.

problemBullets: Risk findings are not stored on the contract record

hero: Set lead times for expiration and termination notices. Keelstar reminds contract owners before work continues without valid terms.
problem: Expired vendor contracts still govern POs because nobody tracked the end date.
problemBullets: Expired agreements remain in procurement catalogs

Auditors find unsigned renewals

metaDescription: Vendor contract management for MSAs, SOWs, and amendments with renewal and risk review in one place.

metaDescription: Contract notice period tracking so you terminate or renegotiate vendor deals before auto-renewal locks you in.
h1: Track contract notice periods before auto-renewal
hero: Record how many days notice you need to exit or renegotiate. Keelstar alerts owners far enough ahead to act.
problem: Teams intend to renegotiate but miss the 60-day notice window buried in section 12.
problemBullets: Notice rules are not extracted from contracts

metaDescription: Contract review checklist for vendor agreements covering insurance, indemnity, data use, and termination rights.

problemBullets: Checklists are informal or attorney-specific

problemBullets: Queues are invisible outside AP clerks

metaDescription: Policy acknowledgment tracking with timestamps, reminders, and completion reporting for HR and compliance.

hero: Distribute policies through tracked links and prove who acknowledged which version and when.
problem: Sign-in sheets and LMS exports do not tie acknowledgments to the exact policy version employees saw.
problemBullets: Version drift when policies update mid-cycle
Remote staff never complete paper attestations
Completion reports are manual pivot tables
Investigations lack proof of acknowledgment

metaDescription: Employee policy acknowledgment workflows with reminders for staff who have not attested.
h1: Employee policy acknowledgment without paper binders
hero: Send employees a secure link to read and attest to current policies. Remind stragglers automatically.
problem: HR emails PDF policies and asks for reply-all confirmations that are impossible to audit.
problemBullets: Reply-all attestations are incomplete
Employees attest to outdated attachments
Managers cannot see team completion
Regulators ask for version-specific proof

metaDescription: Policy version control so attestations always reference the current handbook or compliance policy text.

hero: Publish a new policy version, retire the old one, and require re-attestation when material terms change.
problem: Employees acknowledge policies without knowing whether they signed the 2023 or 2025 handbook.

HR cannot prove which text was shown

metaDescription: Policy attestation software for HIPAA, safety, and code-of-conduct programs with audit exports.
h1: Policy attestation software with audit exports
hero: Run attestation campaigns, capture legally meaningful timestamps, and export completion evidence for regulators and internal audit.
problem: Attestation campaigns rely on shared drives and honor-system checkboxes.
problemBullets: Campaigns lack per-employee tracking

Follow-up is a manual mail merge
